summary.narrative: Mayor Levar Stoney completed two terms with a mixed but generally positive record on campaign promises. He made notable progress on education investment and economic development, though some infrastructure and affordable housing initiatives fell short of stated goals.

promises.text: Invest in public education and improve Richmond Public Schools
promises.evidence: Stoney cited keeping promises on public education as a top accomplishment. His administration increased RPS funding and supported school modernization initiatives.

promises.text: Remove Confederate monuments from Monument Avenue
promises.evidence: In 2020, Stoney ordered the emergency removal of Confederate statues citing public safety concerns during protests. All city-owned Confederate monuments were removed.

promises.text: Increase affordable housing options in Richmond
promises.evidence: The administration created an Affordable Housing Trust Fund and invested in housing programs, but affordable housing shortages persisted throughout his tenure.

promises.text: Redevelop the Richmond Coliseum and downtown area
promises.evidence: The Navy Hill development project, a $1.5 billion proposal to replace the Coliseum, was rejected by City Council in 2020 and never revived during his tenure.

promises.text: Improve city infrastructure including roads and bridges
promises.evidence: Stoney's administration passed a meals tax increase dedicated to school and infrastructure improvements, funding road repairs and facility upgrades.

promises.text: Address poverty and create economic opportunity for all residents
promises.evidence: Launched anti-poverty initiatives and workforce development programs. Poverty rates showed modest improvement but remained significant challenges.

promises.text: Reform the Richmond Police Department and improve community relations
promises.evidence: Implemented police reforms including creating a civilian review board and updating use-of-force policies following 2020 protests.

promises.text: Establish a comprehensive transit plan for Richmond
promises.evidence: While the Pulse BRT system expanded under his tenure, broader regional transit connectivity plans saw limited progress due to funding constraints.
